certified_rows.feature_scorecard_summary: Stable when provider-backed work items, local drafts, queued publishes, imported snapshots, and provider-committed state remain visibly distinct.

provider_family_compatibility.rationale: Issue-tracker work items, tickets, and incidents are explicitly modeled as provider-backed objects with local-draft, queued-publish, and provider-committed separation.
Code-host evidence currently covers review-linked change intent and browser handoff, but not full provider-backed work-item detail rows.
CI providers contribute acting-identity and reconciliation proof, but they do not inherit the work-item object-vocabulary claim.

acting_identity_story.rationale: Provider scope review keeps human account, installation grant, delegated credential, browser-only fallback, and denied scope explicitly distinct before any provider-backed mutation proceeds.

offline_publish_later_story.rationale: Deferred publish recovery preserves local draft, queued publish, export-safe packet lineage, and replay review when target or scope drift occurs.

reconciliation_story.rationale: Provider event ingestion and reconciliation keep typed envelopes, replay-ledger lineage, dedupe decisions, and callback-deny audit events visible before imported truth can affect user-visible state.

certified_rows.feature_scorecard_summary: Stable when publish-now, local-draft, and open-in-provider mutation review all keep target, actor, side effects, and fallback posture explicit.

CI providers do not inherit the work-item mutation-review badge because no current transition-review packet exists for that family.
